distinctive_elements: 3D sound effects (first video game to use this technology in Amiga version)
Sound effects used to indicate creature proximity for immersion

notes: SNES version released 1993. Original composer Wayne Holder credited. Detailed music parameters (tempo, keys, chord progressions, area-specific tracks) not documented in available sources. Wikipedia confirms 3D sound effects innovation but does not provide specific audio parameter data. Soundtrack album 'Dungeon Master: The Album' mentioned as released separately with music by Darrell Harve, but track details unavailable.
